3c262986-3032-4372-8832-e613068ac1d0.title: The Shipping Opportunity
answers: It requires significant capital, domain expertise, and operational involvement
Ticket size — large upfront capital requirements
Investors generally cannot exit on demand; transfers require manager approval and legal steps
Decision-making is concentrated with operators, leaving investors with limited visibility into key operational choices
Valuations rely on broker indications and comparable sales that are not always auditable to outside investors
Information is distributed across multiple parties — technical managers, brokers, insurers, legal entities — rather than consolidated
Access is organized through closed fundraising rounds and manual onboarding, limiting the addressable investor base
It provides tangible hard-asset collateral, cash flow through chartering, and exposure to real macroeconomic trade dynamics
Vessel values, earnings, and risks vary significantly by segment, age, specification, and trading pattern
Transfers require bespoke coordination between seller, buyer, manager, and legal parties — making ownership changes slow and uncertain
